ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for tuna mayo bagel sandwich; FOODON_00002473 (sandwich) is the closest defensible broader class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKM (Sandwiches) is the closest defensible category; no specific tuna bagel sandwich code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for tuna mayo bagel sandwich; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one whole tuna mayo bagel sandwich with cheese, approximately 200 g, as commonly sold at Singaporean cafés and bakeries.

nutrient_source.title: Tuna Mayo Bagel Sandwich — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id G00010922 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with provenance 'canonical' and confidence 0.95. All per_100g macro and micronutrient values are taken directly from the HPB record. Saturated fat and sugar are not reported in the HPB record; saturated fat is estimated at 1.5 g/100g and sugar at 1.5 g/100g based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for tuna salad sandwich (NDB 35188) as a secondary cross-reference, documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; bagel (white, plain) GI = 72
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for tuna mayo bagel sandwich. The GI of 72 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white plain bagel (GI = 72, glucose reference), which is the dominant carbohydrate component of this dish. Tuna, mayonnaise, and cheese contribute negligible carbohydrate and are not expected to materially alter the GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (72 × 18.82) / 100 ≈ 13.5, using 200 g serving size and 9.41 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 402 mg/100 g with insufficient potassium offset (178 mg/100 g) and zero dietary fibre
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this sandwich in its standard form; if consumed, choose low-sodium canned tuna, reduced-fat mayonnaise, and a wholegrain bagel, and pair with high-potassium vegetables such as avocado or spinach to partially offset the sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High GI bagel (GI 72) with zero dietary fibre accelerates postprandial glucose rise despite moderate total carbohydrate (9.41 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should substitute a wholegrain or high-fibre bagel to lower the glycemic index, limit portion size to one sandwich, and pair with non-starchy vegetables to blunt the postprandial glucose response; the high protein content (16 g/100 g) from tuna is beneficial for glycaemic moderation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated sodium (402 mg/100 g) and mayonnaise-derived fat offset by tuna omega-3 fatty acids (EPA/DHA) which are cardioprotective
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease management, choose low-sodium canned tuna and reduced-fat or olive-oil-based mayonnaise to lower sodium and saturated fat intake, while retaining the cardioprotective omega-3 benefit of tuna; limit to one sandwich per meal and avoid adding processed cheese high in saturated fat.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Tuna provides anti-inflammatory omega-3 (EPA/DHA); nightshade ingredients (tomato) may trigger flares in sensitive individuals
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Tuna's omega-3 fatty acids are beneficial for reducing joint inflammation in rheumatoid arthritis; however, individuals sensitive to nightshades should omit tomato, choose low-sodium tuna, and substitute mayonnaise with an olive-oil-based dressing to minimise pro-inflammatory omega-6 intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tuna Mayo Bagel Sandwich with Cheese is a popular café-style sandwich widely available in Singapore, consisting of a plain or sesame bagel sliced in half and filled with canned tuna mixed with mayonnaise, topped with a slice of processed or cheddar cheese, and typically garnished with lettuce and tomato. The bagel provides a dense, chewy bread base with a slightly crisp exterior, while the tuna-mayo filling is creamy, savoury, and mildly tangy. Per 100 g, the sandwich delivers 187 kcal, 16 g protein, 9.26 g fat, and 9.41 g carbohydrate, with a notable sodium content of 402 mg. The high protein content from canned tuna makes it a relatively satiating option. However, the white bagel carries a high glycemic index (GI 72), and the absence of dietary fibre (0 g/100 g) means carbohydrates are absorbed rapidly, which is a concern for individuals managing blood glucose. The sodium level is borderline high, primarily from canned tuna and mayonnaise, making it unsuitable for hypertension without modification. Tuna is a source of omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA), which confer anti-inflammatory and cardioprotective benefits. This sandwich is commonly consumed as a lunch or light dinner in Singaporean cafés, bakeries, and convenience stores. Modifications such as using a wholegrain bagel, low-sodium tuna, and reduced-fat mayonnaise can significantly improve its nutritional profile for individuals with cardiometabolic conditions.

Calories in tuna bagel sandwich Singapore

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database (provenance: canonical, confidence: 0.95, review_status: reviewed). Saturated fat and sugar are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy tuna salad sandwich as a secondary cross-reference and are clearly documented. GI is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database for white plain bagel as the dominant carbohydrate component.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.5 g/100g) and sugar (1.5 g/100g) are not reported in the HPB source record; values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for tuna salad sandwich (NDB 35188) and should be verified against a direct HPB or ICMR-NIN measurement when available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, fat-soluble vitamins, B-vitamin panel, and moisture data for this composite sandwich are not reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy tuna salad sandwich entry as of 2026-04-16. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 as reported by HPB for this specific white-bagel preparation; this is a confirmed zero per the HPB source, not a data gap. 4. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on white plain bagel (University of Sydney GI Database); no direct GI measurement for tuna mayo bagel sandwich was found in approved sources. 5. Halal status is assumed based on typical ingredients (canned tuna, mayonnaise, cheese); individual product halal certification should be verified. 6. The description field in the input ('With Cheese') has been incorporated into the canonical food name and classification.
